dealSummary: Manus is in talks to raise about $1 billion in a round that could fulfill demands from the Chinese government for the AI startup to unwind its acquisition by Meta, Bloomberg reported.
Hark, the developer of a personal AI platform, raised a $700 million Series A led by Parkway Venture Capital at a $6 billion valuation.
Cloud computing startup Modal raised a $355 million round led by General Catalyst and Redpoint at a $4.65 billion valuation.
Aerospace and defense components manufacturer Amca secured a $300 million Series B led by Caffeinated Capital.
AI search engine developer Exa raised a $250 million round led by Andreessen Horowitz at a $2.2 billion valuation.
Farther, which operates a wealth management platform, raised a $150 million Series D led by General Atlantic.
London-based Fresha, the operator of a beauty and wellness services booking platform, received an $80 million investment from KKR's Next Generation Technology Growth fund.
India-based Scapia, a fintech startup specializing in travel payments, raised $63 million led by General Catalyst.
Cybersecurity startup Socket, which focuses on the software supply chain, secured a $60 million Series B led by Thrive Capital at a $1 billion valuation.
Paris-based AI-powered procurement startup Pivot raised a $40 million Series B led by Forestay Capital and Notion Capital.
Prelude, a Paris-based digital onboarding startup, secured a $20 million Series A led by 20VC.
